# GitHub Copilot Instructions for `travel-tour`

> This file is used by GitHub Copilot to understand project conventions, setup, and how to be most helpful when working in this repository.

---

## 🧭 Project Overview

- **Stack:** Next.js (App Router), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React 19.
- **Purpose:** A travel/tours site with routing for listings, single tour pages, search, filters, and a small API route for Telegram integration.
- **Location of key UI code:** `app/` (Next.js App Router). Shared UI components live in `components/`.

---

## 🚀 Getting Started (Common Commands)

In this repo, use the standard Next.js scripts in `package.json`:

- `npm run dev` — Starts the Next.js dev server on `http://localhost:3000`
- `npm run build` — Builds for production
- `npm run start` — Starts the production server (after `build`)
- `npm run lint` — Runs ESLint

> Note: The repo uses Tailwind CSS 4 + PostCSS and Next.js 16.

---

## 📁 Key Code Areas / Architecture

- `app/` — Next.js App Router routes and layouts.
- `app/api/telegram/route.tsx` — API route for Telegram integration.
- `components/` — UI components used across pages; `components/home/` holds homepage sections.
- `lib/` — Utility helpers (e.g., `getImageUrl.tsx`).
- `service/` — API wrapper (`api.tsx`).
- `hooks/` — Custom React hooks (e.g., `useTours.tsx`).
- `types/` — Shared TypeScript types.

---

## ✅ Conventions & Expectations

### Routing / Pages
- Uses the **App Router** (`app/`) with file-based routing.
- Dynamic routes use Next.js conventions like `app/[singleId]/page.tsx`.

### Styling
- Uses **Tailwind CSS** (configured in `postcss.config.mjs`).
- Global styles live in `app/globals.css`.

### Images
- Uses Next.js `<Image />` with remote patterns configured in `next.config.ts`.
- Add hosts to `next.config.ts` when adding new remote image sources.

### Types & Data Fetching
- Shared type definitions live under `types/`.
- API calls are centralized in `service/api.tsx` and consumed via hooks like `useTours.tsx`.

---

## 🧩 Helpful Tips

- The app is primarily client rendering, but routes under `app/` may use server/edge defaults. If you need client-side state/hooks, ensure the component uses `'use client'` at the top.
- The project includes a `telegram` API route. When modifying it, verify the function is compatible with Next.js route handler conventions.

---

## ❌ Common Anti-Patterns (what to avoid)

- Avoid mutating props or shared objects in-place.
- Don’t forget the `'use client'` directive in components that use hooks (e.g., `useState`, `useEffect`, `useTours`).
- Avoid adding new runtime dependencies without confirming they are needed in production.

---

## 🧪 Test Coverage

This repository currently has no dedicated test framework configured. If you add tests, update this file with how to run them.

---

## 🔎 Example Prompts

Here are examples of prompts that work well in this workspace:

- “Update `app/page.tsx` to add a new hero section component that fetches featured tours from `service/api.tsx`.”
- “Fix the hydration mismatch error on `app/single/[singleId]/page.tsx` (client vs server mismatch).”
- “Add a new API route under `app/api/` that returns a list of tour categories with caching.”

---

## Next Enhancements (Agent Customization Ideas)

When you want to build a custom agent for this repo, consider:

- **`/create-agent tour-data`** — Agent that understands the Tours domain (types in `types/`, hooks in `hooks/`, API shape in `service/api.tsx`). Can suggest improvements to filtering, caching, and types.
- **`/create-agent next-app-router`** — Agent that enforces App Router conventions, auto-adds `'use client'` where required, and recommends best practices for data fetching.
